pages.title: Platforms & Intents
pages.description: Multi-platform support and communication intent system
pages.content:
CL SDK's agent system adapts to different communication platforms and interaction modes through the platform/intent model.

## Platforms

Five platforms are supported, each with different formatting capabilities:

| Platform | Markdown | Links | Rich Formatting | Max Length | Sign-off |
|----------|----------|-------|----------------|------------|----------|
| `email` | No | Yes | No | — | Yes |
| `chat` | Yes | Yes | Yes | — | No |
| `sms` | No | No | No | 1,600 | No |
| `slack` | Yes | Yes | Yes | — | No |
| `discord` | Yes | Yes | Yes | 2,000 | No |

### Platform configs

Access platform configurations directly:

```typescript

const emailConfig = PLATFORM_CONFIGS.email;
// { supportsMarkdown: false, supportsLinks: true, supportsRichFormatting: false, signOff: true }
```

### Custom platform config

Override the default config for a platform:

```typescript
const ctx: AgentContext = {
  platform: "chat",
  intent: "direct",
  siteUrl: "https://app.example.com",
  platformConfig: {
    supportsMarkdown: true,
    supportsLinks: true,
    supportsRichFormatting: true,
    maxResponseLength: 4000,  // Custom limit
  },
};
```

## Communication intents

Intents describe the relationship between the sender and the agent:

### `direct`

The user is communicating directly with the agent (e.g., emailing the agent address, using web chat). The agent responds as itself with full capabilities.

### `mediated`

The message was forwarded by someone (e.g., a broker forwarding an email from a client). The agent responds to the forwarder, not the original sender. Links to the app are omitted.

### `observed`

The agent was CC'd on a conversation. It observes and may provide relevant information, but doesn't insert itself as a primary participant.

## COI handling

The `coiHandling` field on `AgentContext` controls how certificate of insurance requests are routed:

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `"broker"` | Route COI requests to the broker contact |
| `"user"` | The user can generate COIs directly |
| `"member"` | Route to a specific team member |
| `"ignore"` | Don't handle COI requests |

```typescript
const ctx: AgentContext = {
  platform: "email",
  intent: "direct",
  siteUrl: "https://app.example.com",
  coiHandling: "broker",
  brokerName: "ABC Insurance Agency",
  brokerContactName: "John Smith",
  brokerContactEmail: "john@abcinsurance.com",
};
```

pages.title: Agent System Prompt
pages.description: Build composable, insurance-aware system prompts for conversational agents

pages.title: Tool Definitions
pages.description: Schema-only tool definitions for agent function calling

pages.title: Agent API
pages.description: Complete reference for agent prompt builders and context functions
pages.content:
## System prompt

### `buildAgentSystemPrompt(ctx)`

Build a complete agent system prompt from composable modules.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `ctx` | `AgentContext` | Agent context configuration |

**Returns:** `string`

## Individual modules

Each module can be used independently for custom prompt composition.

### `buildIdentityPrompt(ctx)`

Agent identity and name. **Returns:** `string`

### `buildIntentPrompt(ctx)`

Communication intent behavior rules. **Returns:** `string`

### `buildFormattingPrompt(ctx)`

Platform-specific output formatting rules. **Returns:** `string`

### `buildSafetyPrompt(ctx)`

Scope guardrails, anti-hallucination, prompt injection defense. **Returns:** `string`

### `buildCoverageGapPrompt(ctx)`

Coverage gap detection guidance. Returns `null` for some intents. **Returns:** `string | null`

### `buildCoiRoutingPrompt(ctx)`

Certificate of insurance request routing. **Returns:** `string`

### `buildQuotesPoliciesPrompt()`

Document type differentiation rules. No context needed. **Returns:** `string`

### `buildConversationMemoryGuidance()`

Cross-conversation memory continuity rules. No context needed. **Returns:** `string`

## Context builders

### `buildDocumentContext(policies, quotes, queryText)`

Build ranked document context for a query. Scores and ranks policies and quotes by relevance.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `policies` | `PolicyDocument[]` | Available policy documents |
| `quotes` | `QuoteDocument[]` | Available quote documents |
| `queryText` | `string` | User's question |

**Returns:** `{ context: string; relevantPolicyIds: string[]; relevantQuoteIds: string[] }`

```typescript
const { context, relevantPolicyIds, relevantQuoteIds } =
  buildDocumentContext(policies, quotes, userMessage);
```

---

### `buildConversationMemoryContext(conversations)`

Build conversation history context for the agent.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `conversations` | `PastConversation[]` | Previous conversations |

**Returns:** `string`

```typescript
interface PastConversation {
  fromName?: string;
  fromEmail: string;
  subject: string;
  body: string;
  responseBody: string;
  _creationTime: number;
  threadId?: string;
}
```

---

### `buildPolicyContext(policies, queryText)` *deprecated*

Build policy context string. **Deprecated** — use `buildDocumentContext` instead.

**Returns:** `{ context: string; relevantPolicyIds: string[] }`

## Intent classification

### `buildClassifyMessagePrompt(platform)`

Build a prompt for classifying incoming messages as insurance-related.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `platform` | `Platform` | The platform the message came from |

**Returns:** `string`

## Legacy (deprecated)

### `buildSystemPrompt(options)`

Legacy system prompt builder. Delegates to `buildAgentSystemPrompt` internally.

### `CLASSIFY_EMAIL_PROMPT`

Legacy email classification prompt. Use `buildClassifyMessagePrompt("email")` instead.

## Extraction prompts

These prompts are used internally by the extraction pipeline but are exported for advanced use cases:

| Export | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `EXTRACTION_PROMPT` | Full extraction prompt |
| `CLASSIFY_DOCUMENT_PROMPT` | Document type classification |
| `METADATA_PROMPT` | Policy metadata extraction |
| `QUOTE_METADATA_PROMPT` | Quote metadata extraction |
| `buildSectionsPrompt(start, end)` | Policy section extraction for page range |
| `buildPolicySectionsPrompt(start, end)` | Alias for `buildSectionsPrompt` |
| `buildQuoteSectionsPrompt(start, end)` | Quote section extraction for page range |
| `buildSupplementaryEnrichmentPrompt(fields)` | Supplementary field enrichment |

pages.title: Application API
pages.description: Complete reference for application processing prompt builders
pages.content:
All application processing functions are prompt builders — they return prompt strings for use with any LLM.

## Detection

### `APPLICATION_CLASSIFY_PROMPT`

A static prompt string for classifying whether a PDF is an insurance application form.

```typescript
```

**Expected response schema:**

```json
{
  "isApplication": boolean,
  "confidence": number,
  "applicationType": string | null
}
```

## Field extraction

### `buildFieldExtractionPrompt()`

Build a prompt to extract all fillable fields from an application PDF as structured data.

**Returns:** `string`

**Field types:** `text`, `numeric`, `currency`, `date`, `yes_no`, `table`, `declaration`

## Auto-fill

### `buildAutoFillPrompt(fields, orgContext)`

Build a prompt to match extracted fields against known business context for auto-filling.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `fields` | `{ id, label, fieldType, section }[]` | Extracted application fields |
| `orgContext` | `{ key, value, category }[]` | Organization context key-value pairs |

**Returns:** `string`

## Question batching

### `buildQuestionBatchPrompt(unfilledFields)`

Build a prompt to organize unfilled fields into topic-based batches for user interaction.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `unfilledFields` | `{ id, label?, text?, fieldType, section, required, condition? }[]` | Fields needing user input |

**Returns:** `string`

## Answer parsing

### `buildAnswerParsingPrompt(questions, emailBody)`

Build a prompt to parse free-text user replies into structured field values.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `questions` | `{ id, label?, text?, fieldType }[]` | Questions that were asked |
| `emailBody` | `string` | The user's reply text |

**Returns:** `string`

## Email generation

### `buildBatchEmailGenerationPrompt(batchFields, batchIndex, totalBatches, appTitle, totalFieldCount, filledFieldCount, previousBatchSummary?, companyName?)`

Build a prompt to generate an email asking users for missing application information.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `batchFields` | `{ id, label, fieldType, options?, condition? }[]` | Fields in this batch |
| `batchIndex` | `number` | Current batch number |
| `totalBatches` | `number` | Total number of batches |
| `appTitle` | `string \| undefined` | Application title |
| `totalFieldCount` | `number` | Total fields across all batches |
| `filledFieldCount` | `number` | Fields already filled |
| `previousBatchSummary` | `string` | Summary of previous batch (optional) |
| `companyName` | `string` | Company name for personalization (optional) |

**Returns:** `string`

## Reply classification

### `buildReplyIntentClassificationPrompt(questions, emailBody)`

Build a prompt to classify user reply intent (answering questions, asking for help, etc.).

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `questions` | `{ id, label }[]` | Questions from the current batch |
| `emailBody` | `string` | The user's reply text |

**Returns:** `string`

## Field explanation

### `buildFieldExplanationPrompt(field, question, policyContext?)`

Build a prompt to generate a human-readable explanation of a complex insurance form field.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `field` | `{ id, label, fieldType, options? }` | The field to explain |
| `question` | `string` | The user's question about the field |
| `policyContext` | `string` | Optional policy context for more specific explanations |

**Returns:** `string`

## Confirmation

### `buildConfirmationSummaryPrompt(fields, applicationTitle)`

Build a prompt to generate a summary of all filled fields for user review before PDF generation.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `fields` | `{ id, label?, text?, section, fieldType, value? }[]` | All application fields |
| `applicationTitle` | `string` | Title of the application |

**Returns:** `string`

## PDF mapping

### `buildAcroFormMappingPrompt(extractedFields, acroFormFields)`

Build a prompt to map filled field values to AcroForm field names in a fillable PDF.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `extractedFields` | `{ id, label, value? }[]` | Extracted and filled fields |
| `acroFormFields` | `{ name, type, options? }[]` | AcroForm fields from the PDF |

**Returns:** `string`

### `buildFlatPdfMappingPrompt(extractedFields)`

Build a prompt to map filled field values to percentage-based page coordinates for flat PDF overlay.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `extractedFields` | `{ id, label, value, fieldType }[]` | Extracted and filled fields |

**Returns:** `string`

## Lookup fill

### `buildLookupFillPrompt(requests, targetFields, availableData)`

Build a prompt to fill fields by looking up values from existing insurance documents or data sources.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `requests` | `{ type, description, targetFieldIds }[]` | Lookup requests |
| `targetFields` | `{ id, label, fieldType }[]` | Target fields to fill |
| `availableData` | `string` | Available data to search through |

**Returns:** `string`

pages.title: Extraction API
pages.description: Complete reference for document extraction functions
pages.content:
## Pipeline functions

### `classifyDocumentType(pdf, options?)`

Classify a document as a policy or quote.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdf` | `string` | Base64-encoded PDF |
| `options` | `ClassifyOptions` | Optional configuration |

**Returns:** `Promise<{ documentType: "policy" | "quote"; confidence: number; signals: string[] }>`

```typescript
const { documentType, confidence, signals } = await classifyDocumentType(pdfBase64);
```

---

### `extractFromPdf(pdf, options?)`

Full policy extraction pipeline (passes 1-3).

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdf` | `string` | Base64-encoded PDF |
| `options` | `ExtractOptions` | Optional configuration |

**Returns:** `Promise<{ rawText: string; extracted: any }>`

```typescript
const { rawText, extracted } = await extractFromPdf(pdfBase64, {
  log: async (msg) => console.log(msg),
  onMetadata: async (raw) => await saveMetadata(raw),
  models: customModels,
});
```

---

### `extractQuoteFromPdf(pdf, options?)`

Full quote extraction pipeline (passes 1-2).

**Parameters:** Same as `extractFromPdf`.

**Returns:** `Promise<{ rawText: string; extracted: any }>`

---

### `extractSectionsOnly(pdf, metadataRaw, options?)`

Retry section extraction using saved metadata from a prior pass 1.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdf` | `string` | Base64-encoded PDF |
| `metadataRaw` | `string` | JSON string from a previous pass 1 |
| `options` | `ExtractSectionsOptions` | Optional configuration |

**Returns:** `Promise<{ rawText: string; extracted: any }>`

---

### `enrichSupplementaryFields(document, models?, log?, onTokenUsage?)`

Pass 3 enrichment. Parses raw text into structured supplementary fields. Non-fatal — returns the document unchanged on failure.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `document` | `any` | Document object with raw supplementary fields |
| `models` | `ModelConfig` | Optional model config |
| `log` | `LogFn` | Optional logger |
| `onTokenUsage` | `(usage: TokenUsage) => void` | Optional token usage callback |

**Returns:** `Promise<any>`

## Mapping functions

### `applyExtracted(extracted)`

Map raw policy extraction JSON to persistence-ready fields.

**Returns:** Object with `carrier`, `policyNumber`, `coverages`, `effectiveDate`, `expirationDate`, etc.

---

### `applyExtractedQuote(extracted)`

Map raw quote extraction JSON to persistence-ready fields.

**Returns:** Object with `quoteNumber`, `premiumBreakdown`, `subjectivities`, `proposedEffectiveDate`, etc.

## Merge functions

### `mergeChunkedSections(metadataResult, sectionChunks)`

Merge section chunks from policy extraction. Combines sections and takes the last non-null supplementary field.

---

### `mergeChunkedQuoteSections(metadataResult, sectionChunks)`

Merge section chunks from quote extraction. Also accumulates subjectivities and underwriting conditions.

## Utility functions

### `getPageChunks(totalPages, chunkSize?)`

Calculate page ranges for chunked extraction.

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `totalPages` | `number` | — | Total page count |
| `chunkSize` | `number` | `30` | Pages per chunk |

**Returns:** `Array<[number, number]>`

---

### `stripFences(text)`

Remove markdown code fences from AI response text.

---

### `sanitizeNulls<T>(obj)`

Recursively convert `null` values to `undefined`.

## Options types

### `ExtractOptions`

```typescript
interface ExtractOptions {
  log?: LogFn;
  onMetadata?: (raw: string) => Promise<void>;
  models?: ModelConfig;
  metadataProviderOptions?: ProviderOptions;
  fallbackProviderOptions?: ProviderOptions;
  /** Maximum number of chunk extractions to run in parallel (default: 2). */
  concurrency?: number;
  /** Called after each model call with token usage for tracking. */
  onTokenUsage?: (usage: TokenUsage) => void;
}
```

### `ClassifyOptions`

```typescript
interface ClassifyOptions {
  log?: LogFn;
  models?: ModelConfig;
  /** Called after each model call with token usage for tracking. */
  onTokenUsage?: (usage: TokenUsage) => void;
}
```

### `ExtractSectionsOptions`

```typescript
interface ExtractSectionsOptions {
  log?: LogFn;
  promptBuilder?: PromptBuilder;
  models?: ModelConfig;
  fallbackProviderOptions?: ProviderOptions;
  /** Maximum number of chunk extractions to run in parallel (default: 2). */
  concurrency?: number;
  /** Called after each model call with token usage for tracking. */
  onTokenUsage?: (usage: TokenUsage) => void;
}
```

### `TokenUsage`

```typescript
interface TokenUsage {
  inputTokens: number;
  outputTokens: number;
}
```

### `LogFn`

```typescript
type LogFn = (message: string) => Promise<void>;
```

## Constants

| Constant | Value | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| `SONNET_MODEL` | `"claude-sonnet-4.6"` | Default Sonnet model ID |
| `HAIKU_MODEL` | `"claude-haiku-4.5.20251001"` | Default Haiku model ID |

pages.title: PDF Operations API
pages.description: Complete reference for PDF utility, form filling, and text overlay functions
pages.content:
## Page utilities

### `extractPageRange(pdfBase64, startPage, endPage)`

Extract a page range from a PDF and return as base64. Useful for reducing API token usage by only sending relevant pages.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdfBase64` | `string` | Full PDF as base64 string |
| `startPage` | `number` | First page to include (1-indexed) |
| `endPage` | `number` | Last page to include (1-indexed, clamped to total pages) |

**Returns:** `Promise<string>` — base64 string of the trimmed PDF, or the original if the range covers all pages.

```typescript

const pageCount = await getPdfPageCount(pdfBase64);
// Only send the first 10 pages to reduce token usage
const trimmed = await extractPageRange(pdfBase64, 1, Math.min(10, pageCount));
```

---

### `getPdfPageCount(pdfBase64)`

Get the page count of a PDF without fully parsing it.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdfBase64` | `string` | PDF as base64 string |

**Returns:** `Promise<number>`

---

## AcroForm

### `getAcroFormFields(pdfDoc)`

Enumerate all AcroForm fields from a PDF document.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdfDoc` | `PDFDocument` | A pdf-lib `PDFDocument` instance |

**Returns:** `AcroFormFieldInfo[]` — empty array if no form fields exist.

```typescript
interface AcroFormFieldInfo {
  name: string;
  type: "text" | "checkbox" | "dropdown" | "radio";
  options?: string[];  // For dropdown and radio fields
}
```

---

### `fillAcroForm(pdfBytes, mappings)`

Fill AcroForm fields by name and flatten the form.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdfBytes` | `Uint8Array` | Raw PDF bytes |
| `mappings` | `FieldMapping[]` | Field name to value mappings |

**Returns:** `Promise<Uint8Array>` — the filled and flattened PDF bytes.

```typescript
interface FieldMapping {
  acroFormName: string;
  value: string;
}
```

**Checkbox values:** `"yes"`, `"true"`, `"x"`, `"checked"`, `"on"` → checked. Anything else → unchecked.

Missing fields and invalid dropdown/radio values are silently skipped.

## Text overlay

### `overlayTextOnPdf(pdfBytes, overlays)`

Overlay text at specified coordinates on a flat PDF.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `pdfBytes` | `Uint8Array` | Raw PDF bytes |
| `overlays` | `TextOverlay[]` | Text overlays to apply |

**Returns:** `Promise<Uint8Array>` — the modified PDF bytes.

```typescript
interface TextOverlay {
  page: number;         // 0-indexed page number
  x: number;            // percentage from left edge (0-100)
  y: number;            // percentage from top edge (0-100)
  text: string;
  fontSize?: number;    // default: 10
  isCheckmark?: boolean; // draws "X" instead of text
}
```

Uses Helvetica font. Overlays targeting non-existent pages are skipped.

pages.title: Type Reference
pages.description: Complete reference for all exported TypeScript types

pages.title: Application Processing
pages.description: Prompt builders for insurance application detection, field extraction, and form filling

pages.title: PDF Operations
pages.description: Page utilities, AcroForm filling, and text overlay for PDFs

pages.title: Changelog
pages.description: Release history for @claritylabs/cl-sdk
pages.content:
## v6.0.0 — 2026-04-09

This release simplifies PDF content format handling by removing automatic detection logic. Users must now explicitly specify how PDFs should be processed instead of relying on automatic provider detection.

### Changed
- **`PdfContentFormat` type**: Removed `"auto"` option. Now accepts only `"file"` (native PDF support) or `"image"` (rasterized fallback). The default format is `"file"`, which works with most modern providers including Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, Mistral, and Bedrock.
- **`ClassifyOptions.pdfContentFormat`**: Simplified documentation to remove auto-detection behavior explanation
- **`ExtractOptions.pdfContentFormat`**: Simplified documentation to remove auto-detection behavior explanation
- **`ExtractSectionsOptions.pdfContentFormat`**: Simplified documentation to remove auto-detection behavior explanation

### Removed
- **`supportsNativePdf()` function**: Removed function that checked native PDF support for a LanguageModel
- **`isAnthropicModel()` function**: Removed deprecated function for checking Anthropic model type

## v5.0.0 — 2026-04-09

This release introduces a provider-agnostic approach to native PDF support detection and expands PDF format compatibility to include Google's native format alongside Anthropic's.

### Added

- `supportsNativePdf` — New utility function to check if a LanguageModel is from a provider with vetted native PDF support, providing a provider-agnostic alternative to provider-specific checks

### Changed

- `PdfContentFormat` type — Updated to support both Anthropic and Google native PDF formats (previously Anthropic-only); documentation comments updated to reflect the generic `"file"` format rather than provider-specific references
- `isAnthropicModel` function — Now marked as `@deprecated`; migrate to `supportsNativePdf` for provider detection use cases

## v4.0.0 — 2026-04-09

This release removes support for text-based PDF content formatting, streamlining the PDF content handling pipeline to prioritize image-based and Anthropic file-based formats.

### Changed

- **PdfContentFormat** — Removed `"text"` option from the union type. The type now only supports `"auto"`, `"anthropic-file"`, and `"image"`. When using `"auto"` format with non-Anthropic models, the SDK now falls back to `"image"` format (requiring a `convertPdfToImages` callback) instead of falling back to text as a final option.

### Removed

- **PdfContentFormat** — Removed `"text"` format option. Text-based PDF content is no longer supported.

## v3.1.1 — 2026-04-09

This release introduces a flexible token limit override mechanism for extraction operations. A new `TokenLimits` type and `resolveTokenLimits()` function enable per-role token limit customization, while the legacy `MODEL_TOKEN_LIMITS` constant is deprecated in favor of the new `DEFAULT_TOKEN_LIMITS`.

### Added

- **DEFAULT_TOKEN_LIMITS** — New constant exporting default token limits per extraction role
- **TokenLimits** — New type for optional token limit overrides per role
- **resolveTokenLimits()** — New function to resolve token limits with overrides merged over defaults

### Changed

- **MODEL_TOKEN_LIMITS** — Now marked as `@deprecated`, replaced by `DEFAULT_TOKEN_LIMITS`
- **ClassifyOptions** — Added optional `tokenLimits?: TokenLimits` field
- **ExtractOptions** — Added optional `tokenLimits?: TokenLimits` field
- **ExtractSectionsOptions** — Added optional `tokenLimits?: TokenLimits` field
- **enrichSupplementaryFields()** — Added `tokenLimits?: TokenLimits` parameter

## v3.0.0 — 2026-04-07

This release introduces enhanced PDF content format handling with support for multiple processing strategies, including automatic detection for Anthropic models. New utility functions and configuration options enable developers to optimize PDF extraction and classification workflows.

### Added

- `isAnthropicModel()` — Function to detect if a LanguageModel is from Anthropic based on provider ID
- `PdfContentFormat` — Type for PDF content format options ("auto" | "anthropic-file" | "image" | "text")
- `ConvertPdfToImagesFn` — Callback function type to convert PDF pages to base64-encoded images

### Changed

- `ClassifyOptions` interface — Added optional fields:
  - `pdfContentFormat?: PdfContentFormat`
  - `convertPdfToImages?: ConvertPdfToImagesFn`

- `ExtractOptions` interface — Added optional fields:
  - `pdfContentFormat?: PdfContentFormat`
  - `convertPdfToImages?: ConvertPdfToImagesFn`

- `ExtractSectionsOptions` interface — Added optional fields:
  - `pdfContentFormat?: PdfContentFormat`
  - `convertPdfToImages?: ConvertPdfToImagesFn`

- `classifyDocumentType()` function — Enhanced to support multiple PDF content formats with auto-detection for Anthropic models

- `extractFromPdf()` function — Enhanced to support multiple PDF content formats

- `extractSectionsOnly()` function — Enhanced to support multiple PDF content formats

- `extractQuoteFromPdf()` function — Enhanced to support multiple PDF content formats

## v2.0.0 — 2026-04-07

This release introduces support for continuous and open-ended insurance policies, strengthens the API by making previously optional parameters required, and removes default model constants to give consumers full control over model provider selection.

### Added

- **`PolicyTermType`** — New type for distinguishing policy term structures: `"fixed" | "continuous"`
- **`PolicyDocument.policyTermType`** — Optional field to indicate whether a policy has a fixed term or continuous coverage
- **`PolicyDocument.nextReviewDate`** — Optional field for tracking the next scheduled policy review or renewal date
- **`METADATA_PROMPT`** — Enhanced with documentation and handling for continuous/open-ended policies with no fixed expiration dates

### Changed

- **`PolicyDocument.expirationDate`** — Now optional (previously required); set to `null` for continuous policies without fixed expiration
- **`enrichSupplementaryFields()`** — `models` parameter is now required (previously optional)
- **`classifyDocumentType()`** — `options` parameter is now required (previously optional)
- **`extractFromPdf()`** — `options` parameter is now required (previously optional)
- **`extractSectionsOnly()`** — `options` parameter is now required (previously optional)
- **`extractQuoteFromPdf()`** — `options` parameter is now required (previously optional)
- **Extraction pipeline documentation** — Updated to clarify that consumers must provide their own models; no default provider is assumed

### Removed

- **`SONNET_MODEL`** — Constant `"claude-sonnet-4.6"` removed
- **`HAIKU_MODEL`** — Constant `"claude-haiku-4.5.20251001"` removed
- **`createDefaultModelConfig()`** — Function removed from exports

## v1.4.0 — 2026-04-03

Major expansion of CL-0 SDK to support personal lines insurance policies. Introduces comprehensive type definitions and structured declarations for homeowners, auto, flood, earthquake, watercraft, and specialty coverages. Expands `PolicyType` from 22 to 42 values and deprecates legacy untyped fields in favor of a discriminated union `Declarations` type.

### Added

**Type Definitions**
- `HomeownersFormType` — Union type for homeowners form codes (HO-3, HO-5, HO-4, HO-6, HO-7, HO-8)
- `DwellingFireFormType` — Union type for dwelling fire form codes (DP-1, DP-2, DP-3)
- `FloodZone` — Union type for NFIP flood zone designations
- `ConstructionType` — Union type for dwelling construction types
- `RoofType` — Union type for roof material types
- `FoundationType` — Union type for foundation types
- `PersonalAutoUsage` — Union type for personal auto usage classifications
- `LossSettlement` — Union type for loss settlement methods
- `BoatType` — Union type for watercraft types
- `RVType` — Union type for recreational vehicle types
- `ScheduledItemCategory` — Union type for personal articles categories
- `TitlePolicyType` — Union type for title insurance policy types
- `PetSpecies` — Union type for pet species

**Declarations Interfaces**
- `HomeownersDeclarations` — Structured homeowners policy declarations with Coverage A-F
- `PersonalAutoDeclarations` — Structured personal auto declarations with vehicles and drivers
- `DwellingFireDeclarations` — Structured dwelling fire declarations
- `FloodDeclarations` — Structured flood insurance declarations (NFIP and private)
- `EarthquakeDeclarations` — Structured earthquake declarations
- `PersonalUmbrellaDeclarations` — Structured personal umbrella declarations
- `PersonalArticlesDeclarations` — Structured personal articles and scheduled items declarations
- `WatercraftDeclarations` — Structured watercraft and boat declarations
- `RecreationalVehicleDeclarations` — Structured RV declarations
- `FarmRanchDeclarations` — Structured farm and ranch declarations
- `TitleDeclarations` — Structured title insurance declarations
- `PetDeclarations` — Structured pet insurance declarations
- `TravelDeclarations` — Structured travel insurance declarations
- `IdentityTheftDeclarations` — Structured identity theft insurance declarations
- `GLDeclarations` — Structured general liability declarations
- `CommercialPropertyDeclarations` — Structured commercial property declarations
- `CommercialAutoDeclarations` — Structured commercial auto declarations
- `WorkersCompDeclarations` — Structured workers compensation declarations
- `UmbrellaExcessDeclarations` — Structured umbrella and excess declarations
- `ProfessionalLiabilityDeclarations` — Structured professional liability declarations
- `CyberDeclarations` — Structured cyber insurance declarations
- `DODeclarations` — Structured directors and officers declarations
- `CrimeDeclarations` — Structured crime insurance declarations
- `DwellingDetails` — Shared dwelling characteristics for homeowners, dwelling fire, and farm/ranch
- `DriverRecord` — Personal auto driver details with violations and accidents
- `PersonalVehicleDetails` — Personal auto vehicle details
- `Declarations` — Discriminated union type encompassing all line-specific declarations variants

**Utility Functions**
- `buildPersonalLinesHint()` — Build context hint for personal lines extraction based on detected `policyType`

### Changed

- `PolicyType` — Expanded from 22 to 42 values to support personal lines: homeowners_ho3, homeowners_ho5, renters_ho4, condo_ho6, dwelling_fire, mobile_home, personal_auto, personal_umbrella, flood_nfip, flood_private, earthquake, personal_inland_marine, watercraft, recreational_vehicle, farm_ranch, pet, travel, identity_theft, title
- `RatingBasisType` — Added dwelling_value, vehicle_value, and contents_value to support personal lines
- `EntityType` — Added "individual" and "married_couple" for personal lines support
- `ContextKeyMapping.category` — Added new categories: "property_info", "driver_info", "vehicle_info", "pet_info"
- `BaseDocument.limits` — Marked `@deprecated`; use `declarations` instead
- `BaseDocument.deductibles` — Marked `@deprecated`; use `declarations` instead
- `BaseDocument.locations` — Marked `@deprecated`; use `declarations` instead
- `BaseDocument.vehicles` — Marked `@deprecated`; use `declarations` instead
- `BaseDocument.classifications` — Marked `@deprecated`; use `declarations` instead
- `BaseDocument.declarations` — Now available as typed discriminated union for line-specific structured data (v1.3+)
- `CLASSIFY_DOCUMENT_PROMPT` — Updated classification signals to include personal lines forms (HO, PAP, NFIP, etc.)
- `METADATA_PROMPT` — Expanded policyTypes array to all 42 lines; added insuredEntityType values for personal lines; added guidance for personal lines extraction
- `QUOTE_METADATA_PROMPT` — Expanded policyTypes array to all 42 lines
All notable changes to `@claritylabs/cl-sdk` are documented here.

## v1.3.0 — 2026-04-03

PDF page utilities and application lookup fill.

### Added

- `extractPageRange(pdfBase64, startPage, endPage)` — extract a page range from a PDF and return as base64, reducing token usage by sending only relevant pages
- `getPdfPageCount(pdfBase64)` — get the page count of a PDF without fully parsing it
- `buildLookupFillPrompt(requests, targetFields, availableData)` — fill application fields by looking up values from existing insurance documents

## v1.2.0 — 2026-04-02

Enriched document model with comprehensive structured data extraction.

### Added

- **Expanded `PolicyType`** from 11 to 22 values — added `fiduciary_liability`, `crime_fidelity`, `inland_marine`, `builders_risk`, `environmental`, `ocean_marine`, `surety`, `product_liability`, `bop`, `management_liability_package`, `property`
- `POLICY_TYPES` runtime constant — all policy types as an array for use in prompts
- **Enriched document fields** on `BaseDocument`:
  - Carrier details: `carrierLegalName`, `carrierNaicNumber`, `carrierAmBestRating`, `carrierAdmittedStatus`
  - Producer/broker: `mga`, `underwriter`, `brokerAgency`, `brokerContactName`, `brokerLicenseNumber`
  - Insured details: `insuredDba`, `insuredAddress`, `insuredEntityType`, `insuredFein`, `insuredSicCode`, `insuredNaicsCode`, `additionalNamedInsureds`
  - Policy metadata: `priorPolicyNumber`, `programName`, `isRenewal`, `isPackage`, `coverageForm`, `retroactiveDate`
  - Structured data: `enrichedCoverages`, `endorsements`, `exclusions`, `conditions`, `limits`, `deductibles`
  - Schedules: `locations`, `vehicles`, `classifications`, `formInventory`
  - Contacts: `insurer`, `producer`, `claimsContacts`, `regulatoryContacts`, `thirdPartyAdministrators`
  - Third-party interests: `additionalInsureds`, `lossPayees`, `mortgageHolders`
  - Financial: `taxesAndFees`, `totalCost`, `minimumPremium`, `depositPremium`, `paymentPlan`, `auditType`, `ratingBasis`, `premiumByLocation`
  - Loss history: `lossSummary`, `individualClaims`, `experienceMod`
  - Cancellation: `cancellationNoticeDays`, `nonrenewalNoticeDays`
  - Claims-made: `extendedReportingPeriod`
- **Enriched quote fields** on `QuoteDocument`: `enrichedSubjectivities`, `enrichedUnderwritingConditions`, `warrantyRequirements`, `lossControlRecommendations`, `bindingAuthority`
- `PolicyDocument.effectiveTime` — time of day coverage begins
- `CONTEXT_KEY_MAP` constant and `ContextKeyMapping` type — maps extracted fields to organization context keys
- `PastConversation` type for conversation memory context
- 30+ new supporting types: `EnrichedCoverage`, `Endorsement`, `EndorsementParty`, `Exclusion`, `PolicyCondition`, `LimitSchedule`, `DeductibleSchedule`, `InsuredLocation`, `InsuredVehicle`, `VehicleCoverage`, `ClassificationCode`, `InsurerInfo`, `ProducerInfo`, `PaymentPlan`, `ClaimRecord`, `LossSummary`, `ExperienceMod`, `EnrichedSubjectivity`, `EnrichedUnderwritingCondition`, `BindingAuthority`, `FormReference`, `TaxFeeItem`, `RatingBasis`, `Sublimit`, `SharedLimit`, `ExtendedReportingPeriod`, `NamedInsured`, `Address`, `Contact`, and more
- New union types: `EndorsementType`, `ConditionType`, `PolicySectionType`, `QuoteSectionType`, `CoverageForm`, `CoverageTrigger`, `LimitType`, `DeductibleType`, `ValuationMethod`, `DefenseCostTreatment`, `EntityType`, `AdmittedStatus`, `AuditType`, `EndorsementPartyRole`, `ClaimStatus`, `SubjectivityCategory`, `DocumentType`, `ChunkType`, `RatingBasisType`, `VehicleCoverageType`

### Changed

- `buildDocumentContext` now accepts separate `policies` and `quotes` arrays (was single `docs` array)
- Updated default model IDs: `SONNET_MODEL` → `"claude-sonnet-4.6"`, `HAIKU_MODEL` → `"claude-haiku-4.5.20251001"`

## v1.1.0 — 2026-04-01

Reliability and observability improvements for the extraction pipeline.

### Added

- Rate-limit retry with exponential backoff on all model calls
- Parallel chunk extraction with configurable `concurrency` option (default: 2)
- Token usage tracking via `onTokenUsage` callback on `ExtractOptions`, `ClassifyOptions`, and `ExtractSectionsOptions`
- `TokenUsage` type with `inputTokens` and `outputTokens` fields

## v1.0.0 — 2026-03-22

First stable release under the `@claritylabs` scope on public npm.

### Changed

- Renamed from `@claritylabs-inc/cl-sdk` to `@claritylabs/cl-sdk`
- Moved from GitHub Packages to public npm registry
- MCP server published as `@claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp` on npm
- Consistent branding: "CL SDK" in prose, "CL-1.0 SDK" for versioned references

## v0.2.5 — 2026-03-16

CI pipeline fix for the docs repo dispatch workflow.

### Changed

- Updated `publish.yml` to use GitHub App tokens for cross-repo dispatch

## v0.2.4 — 2026-03-16

Agent name is now configurable instead of being hardcoded.

### Changed

- `AgentContext.agentName` is now optional — defaults to `"CL Agent"` if not set, replacing the previously hardcoded `"Clarity Agent"`

## v0.2.3 — 2026-03-16

Document link guidance in agent prompts is now configurable.

### Added

- `AgentContext.linkGuidance` — optional custom link guidance string for agent prompts, replacing hardcoded policy/quote link examples

## v0.2.2 — 2026-03-15

CI fix for semantic-release permissions.

### Changed

- Added `issues` and `pull-requests` write permissions to the publish workflow for semantic-release

## v0.2.1 — 2026-03-15

CI fix for Node.js version compatibility.

### Changed

- Bumped Node.js version to 22 in CI for semantic-release v25 compatibility

## v0.2.0 — 2026-03-15

Major refactor: provider-agnostic models, composable agent prompts, and a restructured type system.

### Added

- Provider-agnostic extraction pipeline via Vercel AI SDK `LanguageModel` instances (replaces hardcoded Anthropic)
- Composable agent prompt modules (`buildAgentSystemPrompt`, `buildIdentityPrompt`, `buildIntentPrompt`, `buildFormattingPrompt`, `buildSafetyPrompt`, `buildCoverageGapPrompt`, `buildCoiRoutingPrompt`, `buildCustomerPoliciesPrompt`)
- Platform and intent model (`AgentPlatform`, `AgentIntent`)
- Agent tool definitions (`getAgentTools`)
- Structured type system with `ExtractionResult`, `ClassificationResult`, `ApplicationResult`
- Automated versioning via semantic-release

### Changed

- Models are now passed as `LanguageModel` instances instead of string identifiers
- Agent prompt generation is now composable — individual modules can be used independently
- Type exports reorganized under structured namespaces

## v0.1.1 — 2026-03-14

Patch release with no functional changes.

## v0.1.0 — 2026-03-14

Initial release of `@claritylabs/cl-sdk`.

### Added

- Document classification (`classifyDocumentType`)
- PDF extraction pipeline (`extractFromPdf`, `extractQuoteFromPdf`)
- Application result processing
- PDF operations (merge, split, extract pages)
- TypeScript type definitions for insurance domain models
- CI publishing pipeline

pages.title: Applying Results
pages.description: Map raw extraction output to structured, persistence-ready fields
pages.content:
The extraction pipeline returns raw JSON from the AI model. The `applyExtracted` and `applyExtractedQuote` functions normalize this into clean, typed fields ready for persistence.

## Policies

```typescript

const { extracted } = await extractFromPdf(pdfBase64);
const fields = applyExtracted(extracted);
```

### Output fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `carrier` | `string` | Insurance carrier name |
| `carrierLegalName` | `string?` | Legal entity name |
| `carrierNaicNumber` | `string?` | NAIC company code |
| `carrierAmBestRating` | `string?` | AM Best financial strength rating |
| `carrierAdmittedStatus` | `string?` | Admitted / non-admitted / surplus lines |
| `security` | `string?` | Security/surplus lines carrier |
| `underwriter` | `string?` | Underwriter name |
| `mga` | `string?` | Managing General Agent |
| `brokerAgency` | `string?` | Broker agency name |
| `brokerContactName` | `string?` | Individual producer name |
| `brokerLicenseNumber` | `string?` | Producer license number |
| `policyNumber` | `string` | Policy number |
| `priorPolicyNumber` | `string?` | Previous policy number (renewals) |
| `policyTypes` | `string[]` | Types (e.g., `["general_liability"]`) |
| `documentType` | `"policy" \| "quote"` | Document type |
| `policyYear` | `number` | Policy year |
| `effectiveDate` | `string` | Policy effective date |
| `expirationDate` | `string` | Policy expiration date |
| `effectiveTime` | `string?` | Time coverage begins |
| `coverageForm` | `CoverageForm?` | Occurrence, claims-made, or accident |
| `retroactiveDate` | `string?` | Retroactive date (claims-made) |
| `isRenewal` | `boolean` | Whether this is a renewal |
| `isPackage` | `boolean?` | Whether this is a commercial package |
| `programName` | `string?` | Named program |
| `coverages` | `Coverage[]` | Coverage details with limits |
| `enrichedCoverages` | `EnrichedCoverage[]?` | Detailed coverage data |
| `endorsements` | `Endorsement[]?` | Structured endorsements |
| `exclusions` | `Exclusion[]?` | Structured exclusions |
| `conditions` | `PolicyCondition[]?` | Structured conditions |
| `limits` | `LimitSchedule?` | Structured limits schedule |
| `deductibles` | `DeductibleSchedule?` | Structured deductible schedule |
| `locations` | `InsuredLocation[]?` | Insured premises |
| `vehicles` | `InsuredVehicle[]?` | Insured vehicles |
| `classifications` | `ClassificationCode[]?` | Rating class codes |
| `formInventory` | `FormReference[]?` | All form numbers |
| `premium` | `string?` | Total premium |
| `totalCost` | `string?` | Premium + taxes + fees |
| `taxesAndFees` | `TaxFeeItem[]?` | Taxes/fees breakdown |
| `insuredName` | `string` | Name of the insured |
| `insuredDba` | `string?` | DBA name |
| `insuredAddress` | `Address?` | Mailing address |
| `insuredEntityType` | `EntityType?` | Legal entity type |
| `additionalNamedInsureds` | `NamedInsured[]?` | Additional named insureds |
| `summary` | `string?` | AI-generated summary |
| `metadataSource` | `object?` | Raw metadata from pass 1 |
| `document` | `object?` | Sections and supplementary data from pass 2-3 |
| `extractionStatus` | `"complete"` | Always `"complete"` |
| `extractionError` | `""` | Always empty string |

## Quotes

```typescript

const { extracted } = await extractQuoteFromPdf(pdfBase64);
const fields = applyExtractedQuote(extracted);
```

### Additional quote fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `quoteNumber` | `string` | Quote number |
| `quoteYear` | `number` | Quote year |
| `proposedEffectiveDate` | `string?` | Proposed effective date |
| `proposedExpirationDate` | `string?` | Proposed expiration date |
| `quoteExpirationDate` | `string?` | When the quote expires |
| `premiumBreakdown` | `PremiumLine[]?` | Line-by-line premium |
| `subjectivities` | `Subjectivity[]?` | Binding conditions |
| `enrichedSubjectivities` | `EnrichedSubjectivity[]?` | Subjectivities with category, due date, status |
| `underwritingConditions` | `UnderwritingCondition[]?` | Carrier requirements |
| `enrichedUnderwritingConditions` | `EnrichedUnderwritingCondition[]?` | Conditions with category |
| `warrantyRequirements` | `string[]?` | Warranty provisions |
| `lossControlRecommendations` | `string[]?` | Loss control recommendations |
| `bindingAuthority` | `BindingAuthority?` | Binding authority details |

Quote coverages use `proposedLimit` and `proposedDeductible` instead of `limit` and `deductible`.

## Null handling

Both functions use `sanitizeNulls` internally to convert `null` values to `undefined`. This is required for frameworks like Convex that reject `null` for optional fields. All coverages, metadata sources, and document sections are sanitized recursively.

pages.title: Document Classification
pages.description: Classify insurance documents as policies or quotes

pages.title: Extraction Pipeline
pages.description: Turn insurance PDFs into structured data with the multi-pass extraction pipeline

pages.title: Architecture
pages.description: How CL SDK's multi-pass extraction pipeline and composable systems work
pages.content:
CL SDK is organized into four systems: **document extraction**, **application processing**, **agent prompts**, and **PDF operations**. Each is independent — import only what you need.

## Document extraction pipeline

The core of CL SDK is a multi-pass pipeline that turns insurance PDFs into structured, queryable data.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  PDF[PDF Document] --> P0[Pass 0: Classification]
  P0 --> P1[Pass 1: Metadata]
  P1 --> P2[Pass 2: Sections]
  P2 --> P3[Pass 3: Enrichment]
  P3 --> OUT[Structured Data]
```

### Pass 0 — Classification

Determines whether a document is a **policy** or a **quote**. Returns document type, confidence score, and supporting signals. Uses the `classification` model (fast/cheap).

### Pass 1 — Metadata

Extracts high-level metadata: carrier, policy/quote number, dates, premium, insured name, and a coverage table with limits and deductibles. Uses the `metadata` model (capable).

Supports an `onMetadata` callback for early persistence — metadata is saved immediately so it survives downstream failures.

### Pass 2 — Sections

Splits the document into page chunks (starting at 15 pages) and extracts structured sections with page-level provenance. Uses the `sections` model.

**Adaptive fallback**: if a chunk's output is truncated (JSON parse failure), CL SDK re-splits into smaller chunks (10 pages, then 5), and escalates to the `sectionsFallback` model with higher token limits. Results are merged across all chunks.

### Pass 3 — Enrichment

A non-fatal pass that parses raw text into structured supplementary fields: regulatory context, complaint contacts, costs and fees, claims contacts. Uses the `enrichment` model. Failures here don't fail the pipeline.

## Quote-specific extraction

Quotes run a variant pipeline (passes 1-2) that also extracts:

- **Premium breakdowns** — line-by-line premium details
- **Subjectivities** — conditions that must be met before binding
- **Underwriting conditions** — carrier requirements

## Application processing

CL SDK provides prompt builders for the full application lifecycle:

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  D[Detection] --> F[Field Extraction]
  F --> A[Auto-Fill]
  A --> Q[Question Batching]
  Q --> P[Answer Parsing]
  P --> PDF[PDF Filling]
```

1. **Detection** — classify whether a PDF is an insurance application form
2. **Field extraction** — read every field as structured data (text, numeric, currency, date, yes/no, table, declaration)
3. **Auto-fill** — match fields against known business context
4. **Question batching** — organize unfilled fields into topic-based batches
5. **Answer parsing** — parse free-text replies into structured field values
6. **PDF filling** — write answers back onto the PDF (AcroForm or text overlay)

## Agent prompt system

A composable system for building insurance-aware conversational agents:

```
buildAgentSystemPrompt(ctx)
  ├── Identity          — agent name, company context
  ├── Intent            — direct / mediated / observed behavior
  ├── Formatting        — platform-specific output rules
  ├── Safety            — scope guardrails, anti-hallucination
  ├── Coverage gaps     — gap detection guidance
  ├── COI routing       — certificate of insurance handling
  ├── Quotes/policies   — document type differentiation
  └── Memory            — cross-conversation continuity
```

Each module is independently importable for custom composition. The system supports five platforms (email, chat, SMS, Slack, Discord) and three communication intents (direct, mediated, observed).

## Design principles

- **Provider-agnostic** — accepts any `LanguageModel` from the Vercel AI SDK. Default Anthropic models are lazy-loaded and optional.
- **Pure TypeScript** — no framework dependencies. Works in Node.js, Deno, edge runtimes.
- **Fail gracefully** — early persistence callbacks, non-fatal enrichment, adaptive chunk retry.
- **Schema-only tools** — tool definitions provide schemas without implementations, so consumers control execution.

pages.title: Model Configuration
pages.description: Configure which AI models run each pipeline pass

pages.title: Quickstart
pages.description: Install CL SDK and run your first insurance document extraction
pages.content:
## Installation

Install CL SDK and its peer dependencies:

```bash
npm install @claritylabs/cl-sdk ai pdf-lib
```

Then install a model provider:

```bash
# Anthropic (default)
npm install @ai-sdk/anthropic

# OpenAI
npm install @ai-sdk/openai

# Google
npm install @ai-sdk/google
```

<Callout type="info">
  CL SDK is published on [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@claritylabs/cl-sdk) under the `@claritylabs` scope.
</Callout>

## Extract a policy

The simplest path — default Anthropic models, no configuration:

```typescript

// Load a PDF as base64
const pdfBase64 = readFileSync("./policy.pdf").toString("base64");

// Step 1: Classify — is this a policy or a quote?
const { documentType, confidence } = await classifyDocumentType(pdfBase64);
console.log(`Classified as ${documentType} (confidence: ${confidence})`);

// Step 2: Extract — run the full multi-pass pipeline
const { extracted } = await extractFromPdf(pdfBase64);

// Step 3: Apply — map raw extraction to structured fields
const fields = applyExtracted(extracted);
console.log(fields.carrier);        // "Hartford"
console.log(fields.policyNumber);   // "GL-2024-001234"
console.log(fields.coverages);      // [{ name: "General Liability", limit: "$1,000,000", ... }]
```

## Extract a quote

Quotes have a separate pipeline that captures quote-specific fields like subjectivities and premium breakdowns:

```typescript

const { extracted } = await extractQuoteFromPdf(pdfBase64);
const fields = applyExtractedQuote(extracted);

console.log(fields.quoteNumber);          // "Q-2024-5678"
console.log(fields.premiumBreakdown);     // [{ line: "GL", amount: "$5,200" }, ...]
console.log(fields.subjectivities);       // [{ description: "Loss runs required", ... }]
```

## Use a custom model

Bring any model from any provider:

```typescript

const openai = createOpenAI();
const { extracted } = await extractFromPdf(pdfBase64, {
  models: createUniformModelConfig(openai("gpt-4o")),
  metadataProviderOptions: {},  // disable Anthropic-specific thinking
});
```

## Add logging

Every pipeline function accepts a `log` callback:

```typescript
const { extracted } = await extractFromPdf(pdfBase64, {
  log: async (msg) => console.log(`[cl-sdk] ${msg}`),
});
```

Output:

```
[cl-sdk] Pass 1: Extracting metadata...
[cl-sdk] Calling model (max 4096 tokens)...
[cl-sdk] 12450 in / 2300 out tokens (3.2s)
[cl-sdk] Document: 45 page(s)
[cl-sdk] Pass 2: Extracting sections pages 1–15...
...
```

## Next steps

<Cards>
  <Card title="Architecture" description="Learn how the multi-pass pipeline works." href="/docs/getting-started/architecture" />
  <Card title="Model configuration" description="Fine-tune which model runs each pass." href="/docs/getting-started/models" />
</Cards>

pages.title: CL SDK
pages.description: An SDK for AI agents working with insurance
pages.content:
[Clarity Labs](https://claritylabs.inc) is an applied AI research lab building the infrastructure for AI to work safely with insurance.

CL SDK is an SDK for AI agents working with insurance. Any product or agent can import it to understand, reason about, and act on insurance. Provider-agnostic — works with Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, or any model via the [Vercel AI SDK](https://sdk.vercel.ai).

## Capabilities

<Cards>
  <Card title="Document Extraction" description="Multi-pass pipeline that turns insurance PDFs into structured data — coverages, limits, exclusions, sections — with page-level provenance." />
  <Card title="Agent System" description="Composable prompt modules for building insurance-aware conversational agents across email, chat, SMS, Slack, and Discord." />
  <Card title="Application Processing" description="Prompt builders for detecting application forms, extracting fields, auto-filling from context, batching questions, and filling PDFs." />
</Cards>

## Install

```bash
npm install @claritylabs/cl-sdk ai pdf-lib
```

```typescript

const { documentType } = await classifyDocumentType(pdfBase64);
const { extracted } = await extractFromPdf(pdfBase64);
const fields = applyExtracted(extracted);
```

## Get started

<Cards>
  <Card title="Quickstart" description="Install CL SDK and run your first extraction in minutes." href="/docs/getting-started/quickstart" />
  <Card title="Architecture" description="Understand the multi-pass extraction pipeline and how components fit together." href="/docs/getting-started/architecture" />
  <Card title="Model Configuration" description="Configure models per pipeline pass — or bring any provider." href="/docs/getting-started/models" />
  <Card title="API Reference" description="Full reference for every exported function and type." href="/docs/api-reference/extraction" />
</Cards>

pages.title: Overview
pages.description: Search and read CL SDK documentation directly from AI coding assistants via MCP
pages.content:
The CL SDK MCP server provides documentation search and reference as [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) tools. This lets AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf look up SDK docs while helping you write integration code.

No API keys or configuration required.

## What it provides

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `search_docs` | Full-text search across all doc pages, returns top 5 matches with context |
| `read_doc_page` | Read a specific doc page by slug (e.g. `getting-started/quickstart`) |
| `list_doc_sections` | List all sections and pages in the doc tree |

## Quick start

Add the server to your MCP client config. No install needed — `npx` fetches it on demand.

<Callout type="info">
  Published on [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp) under the `@claritylabs` scope. Source on [GitHub](https://github.com/claritylabs-inc/cl-sdk-docs/tree/main/mcp).
</Callout>

## Architecture

The server bundles all documentation as `docs-bundle.json` at publish time. At runtime it's a lightweight MCP server with zero external dependencies beyond the MCP SDK. It supports two transport modes:

- **stdio** (default) — for local clients like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf
- **Streamable HTTP** (`--http` flag) — for remote clients like Claude.ai custom connectors

```
┌──────────────────┐          ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│  Local clients   │  stdio   │  @claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp        │
│                  │◄────────►│                                 │
│  Claude Code     │          │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐ │
│  Cursor          │          │  │ search_docs                 │ │
│  Windsurf        │          │  │ read_doc_page               │ │
│                  │          │  │ list_doc_sections           │ │
├──────────────────┤          │  └─────────────┬───────────────┘ │
│  Remote clients  │  HTTP    │                │                 │
│                  │◄────────►│                ▼                 │
│  Claude.ai       │ POST/mcp │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐ │
│  Custom apps     │          │  │ docs-bundle.json            │ │
└──────────────────┘          │  └─────────────────────────────┘ │
                              └─────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Client setup

### Claude Code

Add to your project's `.claude/mcp.json` or global `~/.claude/mcp.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "cl-sdk": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Cursor / Windsurf

Add to your MCP settings (`~/.cursor/mcp.json` or equivalent):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "cl-sdk": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Claude.ai (remote)

To connect via Claude.ai's **custom connectors**, deploy the server in HTTP mode and point Claude at the URL.

1. Start the server with HTTP transport:

```bash
npx @claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp --http
```

This starts a Streamable HTTP server on port 8787 (configurable via `PORT` env var). The MCP endpoint is `POST /mcp`.

2. In Claude.ai, go to **Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector** and enter your server URL (e.g. `https://your-host.example.com/mcp`).

<Callout type="info">
  The HTTP server is stateless and auth-free — each request gets a fresh server instance. For production, deploy behind a reverse proxy with TLS.
</Callout>

### Self-hosting

Run the HTTP server with Docker or any Node.js hosting:

```bash
# Direct
PORT=8787 npx @claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp --http

# Or via npm start in the mcp directory
npm start
```

| Environment variable | Default | Description |
|---------------------|---------|-------------|
| `PORT` | `8787` | HTTP listen port |
| `HOST` | `0.0.0.0` | Bind address |

### Other MCP clients

Any client that supports stdio servers:

```bash
npx @claritylabs/cl-sdk-mcp
```

<Cards>
  <Card title="Tools reference" description="Detailed reference for all 3 documentation tools." href="/docs/mcp-server/tools" />
</Cards>

pages.title: Tools Reference
pages.description: Complete reference for the 3 documentation tools provided by the CL SDK MCP server
pages.content:
## `list_doc_sections`

List all documentation sections and their pages.

**Input**: none

**Output**: JSON array of sections with `title`, `slug`, and `pages`.

```json
[
  { "title": "Getting Started", "slug": "getting-started", "pages": ["quickstart", "architecture", "models"] },
  { "title": "Document Extraction", "slug": "extraction", "pages": ["pipeline", "classification", "applying-results"] }
]
```

## `search_docs`

Full-text search across all MDX documentation pages. Returns top 5 matches ranked by relevance.

**Input**:

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `query` | string | yes | Search query |
| `section` | string | no | Limit to a section slug (e.g. `extraction`) |

**Output**: Ranked results with title, slug, score, and a text excerpt around the match.

## `read_doc_page`

Read a specific documentation page by its slug.

**Input**:

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `slug` | string | yes | Page slug (e.g. `getting-started/quickstart`) |

**Output**: The page title and full MDX content.
